stem: A plumber charges a flat fee for each service visit plus an additional charge for each hour of work. The total charge $C$, in dollars, for a service visit lasting $h$ hours is given by the function $C(h) = 85h + 60$. What does the value 60 represent in this context?

choices.label: A
choices.text: The plumber's hourly rate, in dollars per hour

choices.label: B
choices.text: The flat fee, in dollars, charged for each service visit

choices.label: C
choices.text: The total charge, in dollars, for a 1-hour visit

choices.label: D
choices.text: The number of hours after which the total charge equals $85

explanation: In the function $C(h) = 85h + 60$, this is a linear function of the form $f(x) = mx + b$, where $m$ is the rate of change (slope) and $b$ is the initial value (y-intercept). The coefficient $85$ is multiplied by $h$ (the number of hours), so it represents the cost per hour: $\$85$ per hour. The constant $60$ is added regardless of the number of hours worked — it is the value of $C$ when $h = 0$, meaning it is the charge even before any hours are worked. This is the flat fee charged for each service visit: $C(0) = 85(0) + 60 = \$60$.

distractorRationale.A: Confuses the constant term with the slope: reads $60$ as the hourly rate rather than $85$, which is the coefficient of $h$.
distractorRationale.B: Correct: $60$ is the y-intercept, representing the flat fee charged regardless of hours worked.
distractorRationale.C: Evaluates $C(1) = 85(1) + 60 = 145$, not 60; confuses the y-intercept with the value of the function at $h = 1$.
distractorRationale.D: Attempts to solve $85h = 60 \Rightarrow h \approx 0.71$, misreading the constant as representing a time value rather than a dollar amount.
